content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes three space separated numbers as input.


Prints "**Increasing**" if the numbers are in strictly increasing order,
"**Decreasing**" if they are in strictly decreasing order,
and "**Neither**" otherwise.
Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three space-separated integers a, b, and c.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print **Increasing** if the numbers are in strictly increasing order, **Decreasing** if they are in strictly decreasing order, and **Neither** otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. 20 < 30 < 41, so the sequence is strictly increasing.
Only one test case. 41 > 30 > 20, so the sequence is strictly decreasing.
All three numbers are equal, which is neither strictly increasing nor strictly decreasing.

solution.approach: The key insight is to compare the three numbers directly. If a < b < c, the sequence is strictly increasing. If a > b > c, the sequence is strictly decreasing. Otherwise, it is neither. This approach works because it directly checks the conditions for increasing and decreasing sequences.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total. No precomputation is needed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a constant amount of space to store the input and output.
